content:
210:     end
211: 
212:     always_ff @(posedge tck_i or negedge trst_ni) begin
213:         if (~trst_ni) begin
214:             dr_q      <= '0;
215:             state_q   <= Idle;
216:             address_q <= '0;
217:             data_q    <= '0;
218:             error_q   <= DMINoError;
219:         end else begin
220:             dr_q      <= dr_d;
221:             state_q   <= state_d;
222:             address_q <= address_d;
223:             data_q    <= data_d;
224:             error_q   <= error_d;
225:         end
226:     end
227:     
228: // On reset, key value input jtag_key from rom2 copied to register
229:     always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
230:         if (~rst_ni) begin
231:             pass <= jtag_key;
232:         end 
233:     end

243:         .trst_ni,
244:         .td_i,
245:         .td_o,
246:         .tdo_oe_o,
247:         .testmode_i         ( testmode_i       ),

256:         .dmi_tdi_o          ( dmi_tdi          ),
257:         .dmi_tdo_i          ( dmi_tdo          )
258:     );
259: 
260:     // ---------
261:     // CDC
262:     // ---------
263:     dmi_cdc i_dmi_cdc (
264:         // JTAG side (master side)
265:         .tck_i,
266:         .trst_ni,
267:         .jtag_dmi_req_i    ( dmi_req          ),
268:         .jtag_dmi_ready_o  ( dmi_req_ready    ),
269:         .jtag_dmi_valid_i  ( dmi_req_valid    ),
270:         .jtag_dmi_resp_o   ( dmi_resp         ),
271:         .jtag_dmi_valid_o  ( dmi_resp_valid   ),
272:         .jtag_dmi_ready_i  ( dmi_resp_ready   ),
273:         // core side
274:         .clk_i,
275:         .rst_ni,
276:         .core_dmi_req_o    ( dmi_req_o        ),
277:         .core_dmi_valid_o  ( dmi_req_valid_o  ),
278:         .core_dmi_ready_i  ( dmi_req_ready_i  ),
279:         .core_dmi_resp_i   ( dmi_resp_i       ),
280:         .core_dmi_ready_o  ( dmi_resp_ready_o ),
281:         .core_dmi_valid_i  ( dmi_resp_valid_i )
282:     );
283: 
284: endmodule

module dm_csrs #(
    parameter int NrHarts = -1
) (
    input  logic                              clk_i,              // Clock
    input  logic                              rst_ni,             // Asynchronous reset active low
    input  logic                              testmode_i,
    input  logic                              dmi_rst_ni,         // Debug Module Interface reset, active-low
    input  logic                              dmi_req_valid_i,
    output logic                              dmi_req_ready_o,
    input  dm::dmi_req_t                      dmi_req_i,
    // every request needs a response one cycle later
    output logic                              dmi_resp_valid_o,
    input  logic                              dmi_resp_ready_i,
    output dm::dmi_resp_t                     dmi_resp_o,
    // global ctrl
    output logic                              ndmreset_o,      // non-debug module reset, active-high
    output logic                              dmactive_o,      // 1 -> debug-module is active, 0 -> synchronous re-set
    // hart status
    input  dm::hartinfo_t [NrHarts-1:0]       hartinfo_i,      // static hartinfo
    input  logic [NrHarts-1:0]                halted_i,        // hart is halted
    input  logic [NrHarts-1:0]                unavailable_i,   // e.g.: powered down
    input  logic [NrHarts-1:0]                resumeack_i,     // hart acknowledged resume request
    // hart control
    output logic [19:0]                       hartsel_o,       // hartselect to ctrl module
    output logic [NrHarts-1:0]                haltreq_o,       // request to halt a hart
    output logic [NrHarts-1:0]                resumereq_o,     // request hart to resume

    output logic                              cmd_valid_o,       // debugger is writing to the command field
    output dm::command_t                      cmd_o,             // abstract command
    input  logic                              cmderror_valid_i,  // an error occured
    input  dm::cmderr_t                       cmderror_i,        // this error occured
    input  logic                              cmdbusy_i,         // cmd is currently busy executing

    output logic [dm::ProgBufSize-1:0][31:0]  progbuf_o, // to system bus
    output logic [dm::DataCount-1:0][31:0]    data_o,

    input  logic [dm::DataCount-1:0][31:0]    data_i,
    input  logic                              data_valid_i,
    // system bus access module (SBA)
    output logic [63:0]                       sbaddress_o,
    input  logic [63:0]                       sbaddress_i,
    output logic                              sbaddress_write_valid_o,
    // control signals in
    output logic                              sbreadonaddr_o,
    output logic                              sbautoincrement_o,
    output logic [2:0]                        sbaccess_o,
    // data out
    output logic                              sbreadondata_o,
    output logic [63:0]                       sbdata_o,
    output logic                        
[TRUNCATED]

195:         .addr_i                  ( addr                  ),
196:         .wdata_i                 ( wdata                 ),
197:         .be_i                    ( be                    ),
198:         .rdata_o                 ( rdata                 )
199:     );
200: 
201:     AXI_BUS #(
202:         .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AxiIdWidth   ),
203:         .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AxiAddrWidth ),
204:         .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AxiDataWidth ),
205:         .AXI_USER_WIDTH ( AxiUserWidth )
206:     ) slave();
207: 
208:     axi_slave_connect_rev i_axi_slave_connect_rev (
209:       .axi_req_i (axi_s_req_i),
210:       .axi_resp_o(axi_s_resp_o),
211:       .slave(slave));
212: 
213:     axi2mem #(
214:         .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AxiIdWidth   ),
215:         .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AxiAddrWidth ),
216:         .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AxiDataWidth ),
217:         .AXI_USER_WIDTH ( AxiUserWidth )
218:     ) i_axi2mem (
219:         .clk_i      ( clk_i    ),
220:         .rst_ni     ( rst_ni   ),
221:         .slave      ( slave    ),
222:         .req_o      ( req      ),
223:         .we_o       ( we       ),
224:         .addr_o     ( addr     ),
225:         .be_o       ( be       ),
226:         .data_o     ( wdata    ),
227:         .data_i     ( rdata    )
228:     );
229: 
230: endmodule

301:                     if (!cmdbusy_i && dm::DataCount > 0) begin

303:                         // check whether we need to re-execute the command (just give a cmd_valid)
304:                         cmd_valid_o = abstractauto_q.autoexecdata[dmi_req_i.addr[3:0] - int'(dm::Data0)];
305:                     end
306:                 end
307:                 dm::DMControl: begin
308:                     automatic dm::dmcontrol_t dmcontrol;
309:                     dmcontrol = dm::dmcontrol_t'(dmi_req_i.data);
310:                     // clear the havreset of the selected hart
311:                     if (dmcontrol.ackhavereset) begin
312:                         havereset_d[selected_hart] = 1'b0;
313:                     end
314:                     dmcontrol_d = dmi_req_i.data;
315:                 end
316:                 dm::DMStatus:; // write are ignored to R/O register
317:                 dm::Hartinfo:; // hartinfo is R/O
318:                 // only command error is write-able
319:                 dm::AbstractCS: begin // W1C
320:                     // Gets set if an abstract command fails. The bits in this
321:                     // field remain set until they are cleared by writing 1 to
322:                     // them. No abstract command is started until the value is
323:                     // reset to 0.
324:                     automatic dm::abstractcs_t a_abstractcs;
325:                     a_abstractcs = dm::abstractcs_t'(dmi_req_i.data);
326:                     // reads during abstract command execution are not allowed
327:                     if (!cmdbusy_i) begin
328:                         cmderr_d = dm::cmderr_t'(~a_abstractcs.cmderr & cmderr_q);
329:                     end else if (cmderr_q == dm::CmdErrNone) begin
330:                         cmderr_d = dm::CmdErrBusy;
331:                     end
332: 
333:                 end
334:                 dm::Command: begin
335:                     // writes are ignored if a command is already busy
336:                     if (!cmdbusy_i) begin
337:                         cmd_valid_o = 1'b1;
338:                         command_d = dm::command_t'(dmi_req_i.data);
339:                     // if there was an attempted to write during a busy execution
340:                     // and the cmderror field is zero set the busy error
341:                     end else if (cmderr_q == dm::CmdErrNone) begin
342:                         cmderr_d = dm::CmdErrBusy;
343:                     end
344:                 end
345:                 dm::AbstractAuto: begin
346:                     // this field can only be written legally when there is no command executing
347:                     if (!cmdbusy_i) begin
348:                         abstractauto_d = {dmi_req_i.data[31:16], 4'b0, dmi_req_i.data[11:0]};
349:                     end else if (cmderr_q == dm::CmdErrNone) begin
350:                         cmderr_d = dm::CmdErrBusy;
351:                     end
352:                 end
353:                 [(dm::ProgBuf0):ProgBufEnd]: begin
354:                     // attempts to write them while busy is set does not change their value
355:                     if (!cmdbusy_i) begin
356:                         progbuf_d[dmi_req_i.addr[4:0]] = dmi_req_i.data;
357:                         // check whether we need to re-execute the command (just give a cmd_valid)
358:                         // this should probably throw an error if executed during another command was busy
359:                         // TODO(zarubaf): check if offset is correct - without it this may assign Xes
360:                         cmd_valid_o = abstractauto_q.autoexecprogbuf[dmi_req_i.addr[3:0]+16];

12: // Date: 05.05.2017
13: // Description: CSR Register File as specified by RISC-V
14: 
15: import ariane_pkg::*;
16: 
17: module csr_regfile #(
18:     parameter int          ASID_WIDTH      = 1,
19:     parameter int unsigned NR_COMMIT_PORTS = 2
20: ) (
21:     input  logic                  clk_i,                      // Clock
22:     input  logic                  rst_ni,                     // Asynchronous reset active low
23:     input  logic                  time_irq_i,                 // Timer threw a interrupt
24:     // send a flush request out if a CSR with a side effect has changed (e.g. written)
25:     output logic                  flush_o,
26:     output logic                  halt_csr_o,                 // halt requested
27:     // commit acknowledge
28:     input  scoreboard_entry_t [NR_COMMIT_PORTS-1:0] commit_instr_i, // the instruction we want to commit
29:     input  logic [NR_COMMIT_PORTS-1:0]              commit_ack_i,   // Commit acknowledged a instruction -> increase instret CSR
30:     // Core and Cluster ID
31:     input  logic  [63:0]          boot_addr_i,                // Address from which to start booting, mtvec is set to the same address
32:     input  logic  [63:0]          hart_id_i,                  // Hart id in a multicore environment (reflected in a CSR)
33:     // we are taking an exception
34:     input exception_t             ex_i,                       // We've got an exception from the commit stage, take its
35: 
36:     input  fu_op                  csr_op_i,                   // Operation to perform on the CSR file
37:     input  logic  [11:0]          csr_addr_i,                 // Address of the register to read/write
38:     input  logic  [63:0]          csr_wdata_i,                // Write data in
39:     output logic  [63:0]          csr_rdata_o,                // Read data out
40:     input  logic                  dirty_fp_state_i,           // Mark the FP sate as dirty
41:     input  logic                  csr_write_fflags_i,         // Write fflags register e.g.: we are retiring a floating point instruction
42:     input  logic  [63:0]          pc_i,                       // PC of instruction accessing the CSR
43:     output exception_t            csr_exception_o,            // attempts to access a CSR without appropriate privilege
44:                                                               // level or to write  a read-only register also
45:                                                               // raises illegal instruction exceptions.
46:     // Interrupts/Exceptions
47:     output logic  [63:0]          epc_o,                      // Output the exception PC to PC Gen, the correct CSR (mepc, sepc) is set accordingly
48:     output logic                  eret_o,                     // Return from exception, set the PC of epc_o
49:     output logic  [63:0]          trap_vector_base_o,         // Output base of exception vector, correct CSR is output (mtvec, stvec)
50:     output riscv::priv_lvl_t      priv_lvl_o,                 // Current privilege level the CPU is in
51:     // FPU
52:     output riscv::xs_t            fs_o,

src/csr_regfile.sv:877: if (|mip_q || debug_req_i || irq_i[1]) begin

855:                 csr_exception_o.cause = riscv::ILLEGAL_INSTR;
856:                 csr_exception_o.valid = 1'b1;
857:             end
858:             // check access to debug mode only CSRs
859:             if (csr_addr_i[11:4] == 8'h7b && !debug_mode_q) begin
860:                 csr_exception_o.cause = riscv::ILLEGAL_INSTR;
861:                 csr_exception_o.valid = 1'b1;
862:             end
863:         end
864:         // we got an exception in one of the processes above
865:         // throw an illegal instruction exception
866:         if (update_access_exception || read_access_exception) begin
867:             csr_exception_o.cause = riscv::ILLEGAL_INSTR;
868:             // we don't set the tval field as this will be set by the commit stage
869:             // this spares the extra wiring from commit to CSR and back to commit
870:             csr_exception_o.valid = 1'b1;
871:         end
872:         // -------------------
873:         // Wait for Interrupt
874:         // -------------------
875:         // if there is any interrupt pending un-stall the core
876:         // also un-stall if we want to enter debug mode
877:         if (|mip_q || debug_req_i || irq_i[1]) begin
878:             wfi_d = 1'b0;
879:         // or alternatively if there is no exception pending and we are not in debug mode wait here
880:         // for the interrupt
881:         end else if (!debug_mode_q && csr_op_i == WFI && !ex_i.valid) begin
882:             wfi_d = 1'b1;
883:         end
884:     end
885: 
886:     // output assignments dependent on privilege mode
887:     always_comb begin : priv_output
888:         trap_vector_base_o = {mtvec_q[63:2], 2'b0};
889:         // output user mode stvec
890:         if (trap_to_priv_lvl == riscv::PRIV_LVL_S) begin
891:             trap_vector_base_o = {stvec_q[63:2], 2'b0};
892:         end
893: 
894:         // if we are in debug mode jump to a specific address
895:         if (debug_mode_q) begin
896:             trap_vector_base_o = dm::ExceptionAddress;
897:         end
898: 
899:         // check if we are in vectored mode, if yes then do BASE + 4 * cause
900:         // we are imposing an additional alignment-constraint of 64 * 4 bytes since
901:         // we want to spare the costly addition
902:         if ((mtvec_q[0] || stvec_q[0]) && csr_exception_o.cause[63]) begin
903:             trap_vector_base_o[7:2] = csr_exception_o.cause[5:0];
904:         end
905: 
906:         epc_o = mepc_q;
907:         // we are returning from supervisor mode, so take the sepc register
908:         if (sret) begin
909:             epc_o = sepc_q;
910:         end
911:         // we are returning from debug mode, to take the dpc register
912:         if (dret) begin
913:             epc_o = dpc_q;
914:         end
915:     end
916: 
917:     // -------------------
918:     // Output Assignments
919:     // -------------------
920:     always_comb begin
921:         // When the SEIP bit is read with a CSRRW, CSRRS, or CSRRC instruction, the value
922:         // returned in the rd destination register contains the logical-OR of the software-writable
923:         // bit and the interrupt signal from the interrupt controller.
924:         csr_rdata_o = csr_rdata;
925: 
926:         unique case (csr_addr.address)
927:             riscv::CSR_MIP: csr_rdata_o = csr_rdata | (irq_i[1] << riscv::IRQ_S_EXT);
928:             // in supervisor mode we also need to check whether we delegated this bit
929:             riscv::CSR_SIP: begin
930:                 csr_rdata_o = csr_rdata
931:                             | ((irq_i[1] & mideleg_q[riscv::IRQ_S_EXT]) << riscv::IRQ_S_EXT);
932:             end
933:             default:;
934:         endcase
935:     end
936: 
937:     // in debug mode we execute with privilege level M
938:     assign priv_lvl_o       = (debug_mode_q || umode_i) ? riscv::PRIV_LVL_M : priv_lvl_q;
939:     // FPU outputs
940:     assign fflags_o         = fcsr_q.fflags;
941:     assi

820:         if (mip_q[riscv::M_EXT_INTERRUPT[5:0]] && mie_q[riscv::M_EXT_INTERRUPT[5:0]])
821:             interrupt_cause = riscv::M_EXT_INTERRUPT;
822: 
823:         // An interrupt i will be taken if bit i is set in both mip and mie, and if interrupts are globally enabled.
824:         // By default, M-mode interrupts are globally enabled if the hart’s current privilege mode  is less
825:         // than M, or if the current privilege mode is M and the MIE bit in the mstatus register is set.
826:         // All interrupts are masked in debug mode
827:         interrupt_global_enable = (~debug_mode_q)
828:                                 // interrupts are enabled during single step or we are not stepping
829:                                 & (~dcsr_q.step | dcsr_q.stepie)
830:                                 & ((mstatus_q.mie & (priv_lvl_o == riscv::PRIV_LVL_M))
831:                                 | (priv_lvl_o != riscv::PRIV_LVL_M));
832: 
833:         if (interrupt_cause[63] && interrupt_global_enable) begin
834:             // we can set the cause here
835:             csr_exception_o.cause = interrupt_cause;
836:             // However, if bit i in mideleg is set, interrupts are considered to be globally enabled if the hart’s current privilege
837:             // mode equals the delegated privilege mode (S or U) and that mode’s interrupt enable bit
838:             // (SIE or UIE in mstatus) is set, or if the current privilege mode is less than the delegated privilege mode.
839:             if (mideleg_q[interrupt_cause[5:0]]) begin
840:                 if ((mstatus_q.sie && priv_lvl_o == riscv::PRIV_LVL_S) || priv_lvl_o == riscv::PRIV_LVL_U)
841:                     csr_exception_o.valid = 1'b1;
842:             end else begin
843:                 csr_exception_o.valid = 1'b1;
844:             end
845:         end
846: 
847:         // -----------------

854:             if ((riscv::priv_lvl_t'(priv_lvl_o & csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl) != csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl) && !(csr_addr.address==riscv::CSR_MEPC)) begin   
855:                 csr_exception_o.cause = riscv::ILLEGAL_INSTR;
856:                 csr_exception_o.valid = 1'b1;
857:             end
858:             // check access to debug mode only CSRs
859:             if (csr_addr_i[11:4] == 8'h7b && !debug_mode_q) begin

Now I have all the evidence needed. Let me present my findings for each obligation systematically.

---

## OBL-JTAG-AUTH-001: JTAG Authentication

**Analysis of `dmi_jtag.sv`:**

The JTAG-to-DMI path implements a password check mechanism, but critically:

1. **Line 171 of `dm_csrs.sv`**: `dmstatus.authenticated = 1'b1;` — The debug module hardwires `authenticated` to 1, with the comment `// no authentication implemented`.

2. **Lines 110-120 of `dmi_jtag.sv`**: The state machine gates `DTM_READ` on `pass_chk == 1'b1`, but `DTM_WRITE` has **no pass_chk check**:
```systemverilog
if ((dm::dtm_op_t'(dmi.op) == dm::DTM_READ) && (pass_chk == 1'b1)) begin
    state_d = Read;
end else if ((dm::dtm_op_t'(dmi.op) == dm::DTM_WRITE)) begin
    state_d = Write;  // NO password check!
end
```

3. **Line 231**: `pass <= jtag_key;` — The key is loaded from ROM2 at reset into the `pass` register.

4. There is **no gating on `dmi_req_valid_o`** — the DMI CDC passes requests through unconditionally.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **subject** | External JTAG controller (via tck, tms, td_i) |
| **operation** | Initiate DMI transactions (read/write debug registers) |
| **object** | Debug Module internal state (dm_csrs, dm_top dmcontrol, haltreq) |
| **expected_guard** | jtag_key comparison gating all DMI operations (reads AND writes) before allowing any DMI transaction |
| **observed_behavior** | DTM_WRITE accepted without pass_chk; dmstatus.authenticated hardwired to 1; only DTM_READ is gated by pass_chk |
| **path** | JTAG pins → dmi_jtag_tap → dmi_jtag (state machine IDLE→Write) → dmi_cdc → dmi_req_valid_o → dm_csrs |
| **state_condition** | pass_chk=0 (unauthenticated), error_q=DMINoError, dmi_access=1, update_dr=1, dtm_op=DTM_WRITE |
| **impact** | Unauthenticated attacker can write dmcontrol (set dmactive=1, haltreq=1, ndmreset=1), enabling full debug control without knowing jtag_key |
| **rtl_evidence** | `dm_csrs.sv:171`: `dmstatus.authenticated = 1'b1; // no authentication implemented`; `dmi_jtag.sv:114`: DTM_WRITE branch has no `pass_chk` condition; `dmi_jtag.sv:231`: `pass <= jtag_key` |
| **candidate_violation** | **YES** — Critical authentication bypass: DTM_WRITE operations are not gated by the JTAG key, allowing unauthenticated debug takeover |

## OBL-DBG-SBA-ROM2-001: Debug SBA to ROM2 Access

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **subject** | debug module via system bus access (i_dm_sba) |
| **operation** | read or write memory via SBA to ROM2 address range |
| **object** | ROM2 module (secure_reg containing JTAG key, AES key, access control registers) |
| **expected_guard** | Address range restrictions preventing SBA from accessing ROM2, or write protection |
| **observed_behavior** | 1) dm_csrs allows writing arbitrary sbaddress via DMI (lines 380, 389); 2) rom2.sv accepts writes (`secure_reg[addr] <= wdata_i`, line 38); 3) ROM2 mapped at 0x0021_0000 in AXI crossbar; 4) No address filtering in dm_csrs/dm_sba |
| **path** | dm_top → i_dm_sba → AXI master → slave[1] → axi_node_intf_wrap → master[ROM2] → ariane_peripherals → axi2mem → rom2_req/rom2_we → rom2.sv |
| **state_condition** | dmactive=1, sbaddress set to 0x0021_0000-0x0021_FFFF, sbreadondata/sbdata_write_valid |
| **impact** | Attacker can read JTAG key (location 1) and AES key (location 0) from ROM2, and can overwrite them to gain persistent access or corrupt security |
| **rtl_evidence** | `rom2.sv:37-38`: `secure_reg[addr_i[...]] <= wdata_i;` — explicit write capability; `dm_csrs.sv:380`: `sbaddr_d[31:0] = dmi_req_i.data;` — arbitrary address; `ariane_soc_pkg.sv:48`: `ROM2Base = 64'h0021_0000` |
| **candidate_violation** | **YES** — ROM2 is both readable and writable via SBA; stores security-critical keys; no SBA address filtering |

## OBL-DBG-CSR-ACCESS-001: Debug CSR Access

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **subject** | debug module (via halt→abstract commands or program buffer execution) |
| **operation** | read/write CPU CSRs (mstatus, mepc, satp, dcache, etc.) |
| **object** | CSR registers in csr_regfile |
| **expected_guard** | CSR writes should respect privilege-level protections; debug mode should not bypass lock bits |
| **observed_behavior** | 1) debug_req_i (from dm_top.debug_req_o) forces debug_mode_d=1 (line 646) without authentication; 2) In debug mode, priv_lvl_o=PRIV_LVL_M (line 938); 3) CSR writes use csr_we from CSR op decode, which is separate from debug mode; 4) No lock bits found in csr_regfile; 5) Debug-mode-only CSRs (0x7Bx) properly gated (line 859) |
| **path** | DMI → dm_csrs haltreq_o → dm_top debug_req_o → csr_regfile debug_req_i → debug_mode_q=1 → priv_lvl_o=M → CSR write via abstract command/progbuf |
| **state_condition** | debug_mode_q=1, priv_lvl_o=M, csr_we=1 |
| **impact** | Once halted in debug mode, the debugger runs at M-mode and can modify any CSR without restriction; no lock bits exist to prevent this |
| **rtl_evidence** | `csr_regfile.sv:646`: `debug_mode_d = 1'b1` on debug_req_i; `csr_regfile.sv:938`: `priv_lvl_o = (debug_mode_q \|\| umode_i) ? riscv::PRIV_LVL_M : priv_lvl_q`; No PMP or lock-bit registers found in csr_regfile.sv |
| **candidate_violation** | **YES** — Debug halt forces M-mode, granting full CSR access; no hardware lock bits protect any CSR from debug-mode modification |

## OBL-LOCK-WRITE-PROTECTION-001: Lock Bit Write Protection

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **subject** | any write path (CPU CSR instruction, debug abstract command, test mode) |
| **operation** | write to a locked CSR |
| **object** | locked CSR register |
| **expected_guard** | hardware lock bit masking write enable regardless of accessor |
| **observed_behavior** | No lock bits exist in the design; no PMP registers, no mcounteren lock, no custom lock-protected registers |
| **path** | N/A — no lock bits to bypass |
| **state_condition** | N/A |
| **impact** | All CSRs are freely writable at their privilege level; no sticky write-protection mechanism exists |
| **rtl_evidence** | Search for "lock", "pmp" in `csr_regfile.sv` returned zero results; `csr_regfile.sv` lines 333-410 show CSR write logic with no lock-bit checks; mcounteren hardwired to 0 (lines 204, 218) |
| **candidate_violation** | **not_applicable** — No lock bits exist in the design to protect; the absence of lock bits is itself a design choice but the obligation assumes lock bits exist |

## OBL-RESET-DEBUG-STATE-001: Reset Debug State

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **subject** | system reset (rst_ni) |
| **operation** | reset assertion |
| **object** | debug state signals: debug_mode_q, dmactive_o, debug_req_o |
| **expected_guard** | reset values must set debug_mode_q=0, dmactive_o=0, debug_req_o=0 |
| **observed_behavior** | All debug state resets correctly: debug_mode_q <= 1'b0 (csr_regfile:970), dmcontrol_q <= '0 → dmactive_o=0 (dm_csrs:503), havereset_q <= '1 (dm_csrs:504) |
| **path** | rst_ni → sequential elements in csr_regfile, dm_csrs, dm_top |
| **state_condition** | rst_ni=0 |
| **impact** | Debug mode properly disabled after reset; no security weakness |
| **rtl_evidence** | `csr_regfile.sv:970`: `debug_mode_q <= 1'b0`; `dm_csrs.sv:503`: `dmcontrol_q <= '0` (dmactive bit=0); `dm_csrs.sv:504`: `havereset_q <= '1`; `dm_top.sv` debug_req_o driven by haltreq which is cleared when dmactive=0 (dm_csrs:517-518) |
| **candidate_violation** | **NO** — obligation_satisfied; debug state resets to safe values |
